title: Helium Client
description:
A Custom Client That Customizes Every Aspect Of The Game
Whilst Improving Performance.
body:
# Welcome to Helium Client!
Helium client is a **client** based minecraft mod that allows you to customize the game in **real time**.

### IMPORTANT NOTE:
ALL SETTINGS **MUST BE TURNED** ON VIA HELIUM CLIENT SETTINGS.

![Helium Introduction](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0100abd1c375538bb6d6f27e01aec2eacc4e37a1.png)

## Summary:
Helium client **optimizes** the game with settings such as
  - **Adaptive** Scaling
  - Culling Behind Camera
  - Throttle Particles
  - Disable Map Rendering.

Helium client also lets players **customize** the game!
- Custom Block Outlines
- Complete Color Overhaul of UI / GUI
- Custom Inventory Hotbars.
  And Much More!

You can see the Helium Settings by either

1. Pressing Right Shift / The Selected Keybind
2. Options -> Helium Settings

![A image that says tips and tricks](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ed6834ce726c064905da9b26c0cc78766830319d_0.webp)

# Tips

1. **Enable** Optimizations!
2. Visual Effects Increases FPS!
3. Customize Your UI!

In helium client, You can use Profiles to share your Minecraft Settings OR By Using Others.
![idk how to explain ts](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/92dbe80e565a07d289487cd24677d23d222aed6c.png)
You can save a profile. Then go into the folder. Then send it to someone. They can import it by clicking "Import JSON" or "Import TXT" depending on what format it was exported on.

![helium clients resource pack editor](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/308cf73beb633c29f5b15d44168f4d2a36141808.png)

## **Texture Pack Editor** / Resource Pack Editor
![A Texturepack Editor](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c55230782e4b0e50ee72ca339121a09ee4d0c246_0.webp)

### How it works.
- Create A Pack.
- Rename it to whatever you want.
- Search for any texture and edit it!
- You can import textures if you dont want to edit them with the ingame editor
- You can Change Keybinds for tools Via the Minecraft Keybinds menu.
- Save to pack, Then Apply Changes. Then click Done.

Thanks for Using Helium!
